#starting with the file uploads library(shiny) library(shinymeta) library(tidyverse) library(ggplot2) library(readr) library(lubridate) library(colourpicker) #ui ui <- fluidPage( # App title titlePanel("Uploading Files"), # Sidebar layout with input and output definitions sidebarLayout( # Sidebar panel for inputs sidebarPanel( # Input: Select a file fileInput("file1", "Choose CSV File", multiple = FALSE, accept = c("text/csv", "text/comma-separated-values,text/plain", ".csv")), # Horizontal line tags$hr(), # Input: Checkbox if file has header checkboxInput("header", "Header", TRUE), # Input: Select separator radioButtons("sep", "Separator", choices = c(Comma = ",", Semicolon = ";", Tab = "\t"), selected = ","), # Input: Select quotes radioButtons("quote", "Quote", choices = c(None = "", "Double Quote" = '"', "Single Quote" = "'"), selected = '"'), # Horizontal line tags$hr(), # Input: Select number of rows to display radioButtons("disp", "Display", choices = c(Head = "head", All = "all"), selected = "head"), selectInput("x_var", "Choose X-Axis", choices = NULL), selectInput("y_var", "Choose Y-Axis", choices = NULL), #global plot settings tags$hr(), h3("Global Plot Settings"), colourpicker::colourInput( "plot_color", "Plot Color", value = "steelblue" ), sliderInput( "point_size", "Point Size", min = 1, max = 10, value = 3 ), checkboxInput( "trendline", "Show Trendline", value = FALSE ), sliderInput( "bins", "Histogram Bins", min = 5, max = 100, value = 30 ), selectInput( #themes "theme", "Theme", choices = c( "Minimal", "Classic", "BW", "Light", "Dark" ) ), selectInput( #shape of dots in scatter "point_shape", "Point Shape", choices = c("Circle" = 16, "Star" = 8, "Square" = 15, "Diamond" = 18, "Triangle" = 17, "Cross" = 4), selected = 16 ), sliderInput("plot_alpha", "Opacity", min = 0, max = 1, value = 0.7), selectInput("color_var", "Color by (Optional):", choices = NULL) #second category color ), # Main panel for displaying outputs mainPanel( tabsetPanel( type = "tabs", # Output: Data file #initial table tabPanel("Data Preview", helpText("View the raw data structure and column types to ensure accuracy in dataset."), downloadButton("downloadData", "Download CSV"), tableOutput("contents")), #scatterplot tabPanel("Scatter Plot", wellPanel( h4("Best for Showing: Relationships and Correlations"), p("A scatterplot works best for comparing two numeric variables. It helps identify if one variable is correlated to the other and reveals outliers and clusters in datasets.") ), downloadButton("downloadScatter", "Download Plot as PNG"), plotOutput("myPlot", height = "600px"), #scatterplot output actionButton("showCodePlot", "Show Code for Scatter Plot"), #code button verbatimTextOutput("plotCodeDisplay")), #histogram tabPanel("Histogram", wellPanel( h4("Best for: Distribution and Frequency"), p("Histograms are best for looking at a single numeric variable. They show if the data is normally distributed(bell curve), skewed to one side, or has multiple peaks") ), selectInput("hist_var", "Select Numeric Column", choices = NULL), downloadButton("downloadHist", "Download PNG"), actionButton("showCodeHist", "Show Code for Histogram"), plotOutput("myHist"), verbatimTextOutput("histCodeDisplay")), tabPanel("Box Plot", wellPanel( h4("Best for Multiple Categories of Data"), p("Boxplots are best for visualizing the spread and outliers of numeric varaibles across different categories") ), downloadButton("downloadBox", "Download Plot as PNG"), plotOutput("myBox"), actionButton("showCodeBox", "show code for box plot"), verbatimTextOutput("boxCodeDisplay")), #time trend tabPanel("Time Trend", wellPanel( h4("Best for: Patterns over Time"), p("Time Trends work best with a date/time variable. They help visualize seasonality, long-term growth, or sudden drops across a chronological sequence.") ), downloadButton("downloadTrend", "Download"), plotOutput("timeTrend"), actionButton("showCodeTrend", "Show Code for Time Trend"), verbatimTextOutput("trendCodeDisplay")) ) ) ) ) apply_theme <- function(p, theme_choice){ switch(theme_choice, "Minimal" = p + theme_minimal(), "Classic" = p + theme_classic(), "BW" = p + theme_bw(), "Light" = p + theme_light(), "Dark" = p + theme_dark(), p + theme_minimal() #default ) } # Define server logic to read selected file server <- function(input, output, session) { # 1. Data Loading userData <- metaReactive({ req(input$file1) read.csv(..(input$file1$datapath), header = ..(input$header), sep = ..(input$sep), quote = ..(input$quote)) }) # Update selectors when data is uploaded observeEvent(userData(), { df <- userData() cols <- names(df) nums <- names(df)[sapply(df, is.numeric)] updateSelectInput(session, "x_var", choices = cols, selected = cols[1]) updateSelectInput(session, "y_var", choices = cols, selected = if(length(cols) > 1) cols[2] else cols[1]) updateSelectInput(session, "color_var", choices = c("None", cols), selected = "None") updateSelectInput(session, "hist_var", choices = nums) }) # creating the scatterplot scatter_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- userData() req(df, input$x_var, input$y_var) if(is.character(df[[input$x_var]]) && any(!is.na(lubridate::parse_date_time(df[[input$x_var]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"))))){ df[[input$x_var]] <- as.Date(lubridate::parse_date_time(df[[input$x_var]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"))) } p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[input$x_var]], y = .data[[input$y_var]])) if(input$color_var == "None"){ p <- p + geom_point(size = ..(input$point_size), color = ..(input$plot_color), shape = ..(as.numeric(input$point_shape)), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) } else { p <- p + geom_point(aes(color = .data[[input$color_var]]), size = ..(input$point_size), shape = ..(as.numeric(input$point_shape)), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) if(length(unique(df[[input$color_var]])) > 30){ p <- p + theme(legend.position = "none") } } p <- p + labs(x = input$x_var, y = input$y_var, color = input$color_var) if(input$trendline) p <- p + geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), method = "lm", color = "blue") apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output scatterplot output$myPlot <- metaRender(renderPlot, { scatter_obj() }) #download scatterplot output$downloadScatter <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("scatter-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = scatter_obj(), device = "png", width = 8, height = 6) } ) # creating the histogram hist_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- userData() req(df, input$x_var) req(is.numeric(df[[input$hist_var]])) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[input$hist_var]])) + geom_histogram(fill = ..(input$plot_color), color = "white", bins = ..(input$bins)) + labs(title = paste("Distribution of", input$x_var)) apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output the histogram output$myHist <- metaRender(renderPlot, { hist_obj() }) #download histogram output$downloadHist <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("histogram-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = hist_obj(), device = "png") } ) box_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- userData() req(df, input$x_var, input$y_var) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = as.factor(.data[[input$x_var]]), y = .data[[input$y_var]])) + geom_boxplot(fill = ..(input$plot_color), alpha = ..(input$plot_alpha)) + labs(x = input$x_var, y = input$y_var) apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output the boxplot output$myBox <- metaRender(renderPlot, {box_obj() }) #download boxplot output$downloadBox <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("boxplot-", Sys.Date(), ".png")}, content = function(file) {ggsave(file, plot = box_obj(), device = "png")} ) # creating the time trend processedData <- metaReactive({ df <- userData() date_col <- names(df)[sapply(df, function(x){ parsed <- lubridate::parse_date_time(x, orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"), quiet = TRUE) sum(!is.na(parsed)) > 0 })][1] req(date_col) df[[date_col]] <- as.Date(lubridate::parse_date_time(df[[date_col]], orders = c("ymd", "mdy", "dmy"))) df }) trend_obj <- metaReactive({ df <- processedData() d_col <- names(df)[sapply(df, function(x) inherits(x, "Date"))][1] n_col <- input$y_var # Using the user-selected Y variable req(d_col, n_col) p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[d_col]], y = .data[[n_col]])) + geom_line(color = ..(input$plot_color), size = ..(input$point_size)) + labs(title = "Time Trend Analysis", x = d_col, y = n_col) if(input$trendline) p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", linetype = "dashed") apply_theme(p, input$theme) }) #output time trend output$timeTrend <- metaRender(renderPlot, { trend_obj() }) #download time trend output$downloadTrend <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("trend-", Sys.Date(), ".png") }, content = function(file) { ggsave(file, plot = trend_obj(), device = "png") } ) # creating table output$contents <- renderTable({ if(input$disp=="head") return(head(userData())) else return(userData()) }) #download table output$downloadData <- downloadHandler( filename = function() { paste0("data-", Sys.Date(), ".csv") }, content = function(file) { write.csv(userData(), file, row.names = FALSE) } ) # table code buttons observeEvent(input$showCodePlot, { output$plotCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), scatter_obj()) }) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeHist, { output$histCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), hist_obj()) }) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeBox, { output$boxCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), box_obj())}) }) observeEvent(input$showCodeTrend, { output$trendCodeDisplay <- renderPrint({ expandChain(quote(library(ggplot2)), trend_obj()) }) }) } # Create Shiny app shinyApp(ui, server)
